title: Architectures overview
summary: How major model families differ in how they read inputs, mix information, and produce outputs.
openingSummary: Architecture is the blueprint: it names which stacks read data, which blocks mix context, and whether one network or two handles encoding and generation.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: An architectures overview groups model families by how they turn raw inputs into predictions. Sequence models consume ordered tokens or time steps; convolutional networks scan local patches; graph networks pass messages along edges. Within sequence modeling, layout choices split further: encoder-only stacks for rich input representations, decoder-only stacks for autoregressive generation, and encoder–decoder pairs for translation-style tasks. The overview is a map of those families, not a single trained checkpoint.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Model cards and papers name families before they name weights. Knowing whether a system is decoder-only, encoder–decoder, or state-space helps you pick the right module pages and spot when a product bundles training data with layout choices. The overview also separates taxonomy terms—transformer as a block recipe—from layout terms such as encoder-only versus decoder-only.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: A reader comparing chat, embedding, and translation products can start here: chat models are usually decoder-only transformers; embedding APIs often use encoder-only transformers; machine translation historically favored encoder–decoder transformers. State-space and RNN families remain relevant when linear-time recurrence matters more than full attention.

whereItAppears.title: Where It Appears
whereItAppears.body: Architecture overviews appear in onboarding docs, course syllabi, and internal playbooks before engineers open a specific module page. Search and the architecture index use this page as the hub that links outward to family and layout pages already published in the atlas.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: Architecture overview is not a benchmark leaderboard—it explains structure, not scores. It is also not the same as the glossary architecture term, which defines the word in isolation. Finally, transformer names a block family; encoder-only and decoder-only name how those blocks are wired for a task.
